compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin maintainer, I can see synthesized friction themes and proposed guidance edits in retro
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A new Friction Suggestions phase in /dso:retro that reads .suggestions/, clusters records by theme, and synthesizes actionable guidance edits
**Why**: Collection without synthesis is dead infrastructure. The retro integration is the minimum viable consumer that transforms raw friction observations into actionable improvements to skills, CLAUDE.md, or agent prompts
**Scope**:
- IN: New phase in retro/SKILL.md, reads .suggestions/ from tickets orphan branch, clusters by theme (affected_file, skill_name, or inferred topic), ranks by frequency, proposes specific guidance edits per cluster, graceful empty-state (omit section when no suggestions exist)
- OUT: Automatic application of suggested edits (human reviews and approves), pruning/archival of consumed suggestions (future epic)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, /dso:retro produces a Friction Suggestions section when .suggestions/ contains one or more records, with clusters ranked by frequency
  ← Satisfies: "when .suggestions/ contains >= 1 record, clusters are ranked by frequency"
- When this story is complete, each cluster names the affected guidance file, the observed friction pattern, and a proposed edit
  ← Satisfies: "each naming the affected guidance file, the observed friction pattern, and a proposed edit"
- When this story is complete, the Friction Suggestions section is omitted without error when .suggestions/ is empty or absent
  ← Satisfies: "When .suggestions/ is empty or absent, the section is omitted without error"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Performance] With many accumulated suggestions, reading and clustering must stay within tool timeout ceiling — consider processing only suggestions since last retro run (timestamp filtering)
- [Maintainability] Clustering logic should be simple and deterministic (group by affected_file or skill_name field, fall back to text similarity for untagged suggestions)

comments.body: Done definition update: Suggestion data collection should be added as a new structured section in retro-gather.sh (e.g., === SUGGESTION_DATA ===) rather than having the retro skill read .suggestions/ directly. This maintains the existing separation between data collection (retro-gather.sh) and analysis (LLM).
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Consideration: [Maintainability] Retro synthesis must present proposed guidance edits as proposals for human review only — never autonomously apply them. Suggestions will frequently target safeguard-protected files (skills, hooks, CLAUDE.md). The retro output must explicitly note when a proposed edit targets a safeguard file per Never-Do rule 18.
comments.author: Claude
